items.title: [正面] One of those rare gachas that feels like an actual...
items.summary:
One of those rare gachas that feels like an actual game rather than a flimsy pretense for microtransactions. The gameplay, which takes heavy inspiration from Slay the Spire, is a lot of fun, the combat animations are flashy and satisfying, and the soundtrack does an absolutely incredible job of setting the gloomy and unsettling atmosphere that pervades the whole experience. If you like card games and eldritch horror, this game is definitely worth a look.

Before I talk it up too much though, I need to address the elephant in the room here: the Chat GPT translation is every bit as terrible as you'd expect when going from Chinese to English. Character pronouns seem to switch gender with every instance of use, and some of their names fare just as badly. Card names and effects suffer somewhat as well, but thankfully many effects are color coded, which means they usually aren't too difficult to figure out, and card name variations are generally pretty obvious synonyms.

Somehow I've still found the story to be pretty compelling in spite of these issues, which is good because there's a lot of it, with the game largely structured as a VN. It starts off at a university that trains paranormal investigators before spinning off into a series of interconnected mysteries revolving around a string of disappearances. Thankfully it avoids the narrative trap most gacha games fall into by devoting large chunks of dialogue to selling their characters, with most pullable units being minor characters that you often spend more time fighting against than talking to.

Story mode stages have you moving between nodes to different points of interest, which is honestly a bit clunky,  but thankfully most time there is spent engaging in combat instances very reminiscent of Slay the Spire, which works out well despite the slightly limited enemy variety. Seeing as how it's a gacha game and not a roguelike, your deck customization options once you begin a stage are more limited than in StS, with the game game offering you choices between cards modifications rather than entirely new cards. These give you the ability to inflict status effects with cards that otherwise wouldn't be able to, or to give you various resources when playing them. There are relics as well, but they aren't build defining in the way StS relics are.

Thankfully, team building offers a wealth of options, with each of the factions having unique and interesting mechanics, and the game is generous enough that you'll have a lot to work with even early on. The free starter characters are just as good as the gacha units, and even before events you're looking at an average of over two standard pulls per day in recurring currency with 3% rates and a pity of 30, plus a ton more from the campaign. You also get multiple selectors within a few days of starting, so you shouldn't have any trouble building a team around whatever mechanic appeals to you. Limited pulls aren't too hard to come by either, particularly as you're clearing the campaign, and limited units are set to enter the standard pool a few months after their first rerun.

The way this game handles PVP should be a model for the industry, Not only is it minimally pay to win (and pretty interesting, if somewhat overwhelming at first), but if its not your thing you can skip it entirely without  missing out on anything since it shares reward payouts with the daily run PVE mode.

Normally I don't like recommending gachas, but this game has clearly had a lot of heart put into it and I'd like to see it reach the niche audience it deserves even in spite of its terrible translation.

items.title:
[负面] 장점

1. 독특한 분위기와 이에 어울리는 미려한 그림체
2. 덱빌딩 로그라이크의 재미를 ...
items.summary:
장점

1. 독특한 분위기와 이에 어울리는 미려한 그림체
2. 덱빌딩 로그라이크의 재미를 잘 녹여낸 전투시스템(PVE)

단점

1. 덱빌딩 로그라이크+가챠시스템의 불편한 만남
-캐릭을 중복으로 뽑아서 돌파할때마다 천지차이로 바뀌는 캐릭성능.
그런데 이 겜은 한캐릭 풀돌에 필요한 숫자가 무려 '16장'이다.
똑같은 캐릭터를 16장 뽑으라는 것도 어이없는데,
캐릭에 껴주는 장비도 16장 뽑아야 풀돌이다...

그나마 25뽑에 반천장이 있고,
3~4돌정도에서 성능적으로 타협할 수는 있다지만,
덱을 속성별로 4종류 짜야해서
캐릭을 최소 열명 이상 키워야하는 상황에서는 그다지 위안이 되지 않는다.

스토리 밸런스는 노돌기준으로는 잡몹전투도 목숨을 걸어야 할정도로 하드한편이고,
최소한 핵심캐릭이 1~2돌정도는 되어야 밀만하다.

2. 라이브서버와의 진도차이
-이 게임은 모바일 본섭이 있는 게임으로,
스팀출시는 24년 8월 2일이지만,
캐릭풀이나 이벤트들은 전부 본섭을 따라간다.
따라서 밸런스나 가챠풀도 전부 본섭기준이고,
한정뽑에는 서포터캐릭만 세개가 박혀있다던지
풀돌해야 성능나오는 한물간 캐릭터가 있다던지
뉴비가 뽑아 쓰기에 그리 좋은 캐릭터가 올라오지 않는다.

3. 끔찍하게 재미없는 PVP
-한판돌려보고 쳐다보지도 않아서 뭐라 말하긴 그렇지만,
PVE의 초초초초 하위호환인것은 확실하다.
이벤트도 그냥 한바퀴돌고 소탕하면 끝이라
결국 스토리 다 밀면 남는 컨텐츠는 pvp밖에 없을텐데
아마 스토리 다 밀면 접속도 하지 않을 것 같다.

총평

전투는 정말로 재밌는 편이라
난이도가 어려운 4인팟 슬레이 더 스파이어 혹은
솔로플레이 로그라이크 덱빌딩게임이라 생각하고
스토리 밀면서 나온 재화로 덱 구성하면서
스토리만 밀고 끝낸다면 꽤 추천할만한 게임

하지만 뽑기가 중복 16장 풀돌이라는 불닭이상의 맵기이고,
캐릭+장비를 동시에 뽑아야하는데다가
사실상 돌파 안한캐릭터는 팔다리가 다 잘린 상태이기에
하면서 내내 불편함을 느낄바에는 아예 처음부터 시작을 안하는게 좋다고 생각한다.

items.title: [负面] "Slay the Spire" But a mihoyo-inspired Gacha kinda...
items.summary:
"Slay the Spire" But a mihoyo-inspired Gacha kinda works, however as it stands it seems like it's just a bit of a collect-a-thon regarding the characters. I wish I could recommend this game more but the Chat GPT translation of the chinese is really, really bad.  It gets hard to keep track of characters when they have basically got "All pronouns" and the plot, while follow-able, has dramatic moments taken away by a shoddy translation. I feel like this will get fixed, but in the long run,  the main draw of playing Slay the Spire is that the four characters in that game, have wildly different playstyles and many builds you can run.
Morimens is somewhat the opposite. You have very strict playstyles limited to your characters, and that means a lot of the long term enjoyment of the combat is somewhat locked behind a gacha system.
